maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves features supported on connected displays
This command retrieves features supported on connected displays.

Specifies the serial number of the display(s) to retrieve information for. If multiple serial number(s) are given and multiple displays are connected, return data will only show information for Displays associated with specified serial number(s). If not specified, information for all connected displays will be returned.

Specifies the serial number of the display(s) to retrieve information for. If multiple serial number(s) are given and multiple displays are connected, return data will only show information for Displays associated with specified serial number(s). If not specified, information for all connected displays will be returned.
Generates and saves an HTML report for each connected display with the specified calibration preset to the current directory

This command generates and saves an HTML report for each connected display with the specified calibration preset to the current directory in a file with name format: <SerialNumber>_<CalibrationPreset>_CalibrationReport.html by default unless the value specified in the SaveAs parameter includes a file name.

Specifies the serial number of the display(s) to generate a report for. If multiple serial number(s) are given and multiple displays are connected, only reports for Displays associated with specified serial number(s) will be generated. If not specified, reports for all connected displays will be generated.

Specifies the calibration preset to generate a report for. Valid values are: sRGBD65, BT709D65, AdobeRGBD65, BT601, P3D65, BT2020, EPD, sRGBD50, AdobeRGBD50, DICOM, DesignforwebsRGBD65, DesignforprintsRGBD50, PhotographyP3D65, HDvideoBT709, DisplayP3, sRGB, AdobeRGB, BT709, ITURBT2100, PQ2084HDR10, HLG

Specifies a rooted and fully qualified file path to save an HTML report to. You can specify the path with or without a file name. If file name is specified, file name must end in .html. If file name is not specified, the file name will follow the format: <SerialNumber>_<CalibrationPreset>_CalibrationReport.html
If set to true, the command will open the report that was generated. Default is false.

Specifies the calibration preset to generate a report for. Valid values are: sRGBD65, BT709D65, AdobeRGBD65, BT601, P3D65, BT2020, EPD, sRGBD50, AdobeRGBD50, DICOM, DesignforwebsRGBD65, DesignforprintsRGBD50, PhotographyP3D65, HDvideoBT709, DisplayP3, sRGB, AdobeRGB, BT709, ITURBT2100, PQ2084HDR10, HLG
If set to true, the command will open the report that was generated. Default is false.

Specifies a rooted and fully qualified file path to save an HTML report to. You can specify the path with or without a file name. If file name is specified, file name must end in .html. If file name is not specified, the file name will follow the format: <SerialNumber>_<CalibrationPreset>_CalibrationReport.html

Specifies the serial number of the display(s) to generate a report for. If multiple serial number(s) are given and multiple displays are connected, only reports for Displays associated with specified serial number(s) will be generated. If not specified, reports for all connected displays will be generated.
Invokes a factory reset on connected displays
This command invokes a factory reset on connected displays. Brightness, Contrast, Color, Camera, and Audio features will be set to their default values.

Specifies the serial number of the display(s) to reset. If multiple serial number(s) are given and multiple displays are connected, only displays associated with specified serial number(s) will be reset to factory settings. If not specified, all connected displays will be reset.

Specifies the serial number of the display(s) to reset. If multiple serial number(s) are given and multiple displays are connected, only displays associated with specified serial number(s) will be reset to factory settings. If not specified, all connected displays will be reset.
Sets features supported on connected displays
This command sets features supported on connected displays.
Specifies the serial number(s) of the display(s) to apply changes to. If not specified, all connected displays will be updated.
Specifies the brightness to set to the display. Valid values are between 0 and 100. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer.

Specifies the USB-C port to set to the display. Valid values are: USBCVideoUSB20, USBCVideoUSB30, USBCVideoUSB32, USB3840216060HzUSB20, USB3840216030HzUSB30, USB2560144060HzUSB20, USB2560144060HzUSB30, USB34401440100HzUSB20, USB3440144060HzUSB30, USB1920120060HzUSB20, USB1920120060HzUSB30, USB5120216060HzUSB20, USB5120216060HzUSB30, USB3840216060HzUSB30, USBCAUTO.
Specifies the black stretch value to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, Low, Medium, High

Specifies the color preset to set to the display. Valid values are: UserCalib1,UserCalib2,UserCalib3,UserCalib4,UserCalib5,UserCalib6,UserCalib7,UserCalib8,UserCalib9,UserCalib10,UserCalib11,UserCalib12,UserCalib13,UserCalib14,UserCalib15,UserCalib16,DesignforprintsRGBD50,DesignforwebsRGBD65,PhotographyP3D65,HDvideoBT709,sRGBD50,BT709,BT709D65,BT601,P3D65,AdobeRGBD50,AdobeRGBD65,BT2020,sRGBD65,EPD,DICOM,FullNative,SDRCustom1,SDRCustom2,SDRCustom3,SDRCustom4,SDRCustom5,Warm,Cool,Neutral,Standard,Gaming,Cinema,Multimedia,Photo, HPEnhance,CustomRGB,RetroGamingMode,ColorMatch,PQHDR10,HLG,SDRLowBlueLight,ReadingMode,Vivid,Movie,Night,DisplayP3,GameRemasterMode,ECO,FPS,RPG,RTS,ColorPresetsRGB,ColorPresetPanelNative,ColorPresetCinema,ColorPresetWarm,ColorPresetStandardNeutral,ColorPresetCool,ColorPresetGaming,ColorPresetVivid,ColorPresetCustomColor,ColorPresetHPEnhancePlus,ColorPresetLowBlueLight,Reading,TrNight,TrDicom,TrDicom0,TrDicom2,Text,Custom,MovieorCinema,LegacyPhoto,LegacyGaming,LegacyHPEnhancePlus,LegacyLowBlueLight,LegacyReading,LegacyNight,LegacyTechnicolor,LegacyMultimedia,LegacyWarm,LegacyNeutralorStandard,LegacyCool,Full,sRGB,Rec709BT709,AdobeRGB,Rec601,DCIP3,Custom1,Custom2,Custom3,Custom4,Custom5,Custom6,Custom7,DCIM,Custom1,Custom2,Custom3,Custom4,Custom5,Custom6,Custom7,DCIM

Specifies the volume level to set to the display's speaker. Valid values are between 0 and 100. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer. If connected via USBC, SpeakerVolume will follow SystemVolume despite value specified by SpeakerVolume parameter.
If set to false, the display will not restrict file transfer. If set to true, the display will restrict file transfer.

Specifies the language to set to the display. Valid values are: ChineseTraditional, English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, PortuguesePortugal, Russian, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish, ChineseSimplified, PortugueseBrazil, Arabic, Bulgarian, Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, Estonian, Finnish, Greek, Hebrew, Hindi, Hungarian, Latvian, Lituanian, Norwegian, Polish, Romanian, Serbian, Slovak, Slovenian, Thai, Ukrainian, Vietnamese
Specifies the volume level to set to the system. Display volume is synced with the host when connected via USB. Valid values are between 0 and 100.
Specifes the response time value to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, Level1, Level2, Level3, Level4, Level5, Level6
Specifies the resolution to set to the display. Format of string 1234x1234 where two integers are separated by 'x' and the first integer is the width and the second integer is the height.
Specifies the value of the auto-sleep mode to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, On

Specifies the split screen value to set to the display. Valid values are: SplitScreenOff, PbP, DeviceBridge, VirtualDualDisplay, PiP, PbP1_4, PbP3_4, VMD1, VDD1_4, VDD3_4, VMD2, ToggleSplitScreenOnorOff, ToggleBetweenWSplitScreenOptions, SwapLeftorRightSplitScreenInputs, ResetSplitScreenMenu, PbP1_3, PbP3_1
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) presence detection menu. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the ethernet port menu. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the USB Hub. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the audio menu. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT adminstrator to disable (lock) speaker function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the webcam function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the microphone function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the device bridge file transfer function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Specifies the frequency in Hz to set the display. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer.
Specifies the contrast to set to the display. Valid values are between 0 and 100. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer.

Specifies the active input to set to the display. Valid values are: VGA1, VGA2, DigitalDVI1, DigitalDVI2, CompositeVideo1, CompositeVideo2, SVideo1, SVideo2, Tuner1, Tuner2, Tuner3, Component1, Component2, Component3, HDMI1, HDMI2, DP1, DP2, USBCVideo1, USBCVideo2, USBCVideo1Thunderbolt4, USBCVideo2Thunderbolt4
If set to false, auto input switch value will be set to 'Off'. If set to true, auto input switch value will be set to 'On'.
Specifies the power management value to set to the display. Valid values are: PowerSaverMode, PerformanceMode
If set to false, speaker mute value will be set to 'UnMute'. If set to true, speaker mute value will be set to 'Mute'.
Specifies the built-in audio preset to set to the display. Valid values are: Music, Movie, Voice
Specifies the switch USB value to set to the display. Valid values are: Default, Off, On
Specifies the value of the single power on message to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, On
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the audio menu. Valid values are Disable and Enable.

Specifies the active input to set to the display. Valid values are: VGA1, VGA2, DigitalDVI1, DigitalDVI2, CompositeVideo1, CompositeVideo2, SVideo1, SVideo2, Tuner1, Tuner2, Tuner3, Component1, Component2, Component3, HDMI1, HDMI2, DP1, DP2, USBCVideo1, USBCVideo2, USBCVideo1Thunderbolt4, USBCVideo2Thunderbolt4
If set to false, auto input switch value will be set to 'Off'. If set to true, auto input switch value will be set to 'On'.
Specifies the value of the auto-sleep mode to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, On
Specifies the black stretch value to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, Low, Medium, High
Specifies the brightness to set to the display. Valid values are between 0 and 100. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer.
Specifies the built-in audio preset to set to the display. Valid values are: Music, Movie, Voice

Specifies the color preset to set to the display. Valid values are: UserCalib1,UserCalib2,UserCalib3,UserCalib4,UserCalib5,UserCalib6,UserCalib7,UserCalib8,UserCalib9,UserCalib10,UserCalib11,UserCalib12,UserCalib13,UserCalib14,UserCalib15,UserCalib16,DesignforprintsRGBD50,DesignforwebsRGBD65,PhotographyP3D65,HDvideoBT709,sRGBD50,BT709,BT709D65,BT601,P3D65,AdobeRGBD50,AdobeRGBD65,BT2020,sRGBD65,EPD,DICOM,FullNative,SDRCustom1,SDRCustom2,SDRCustom3,SDRCustom4,SDRCustom5,Warm,Cool,Neutral,Standard,Gaming,Cinema,Multimedia,Photo, HPEnhance,CustomRGB,RetroGamingMode,ColorMatch,PQHDR10,HLG,SDRLowBlueLight,ReadingMode,Vivid,Movie,Night,DisplayP3,GameRemasterMode,ECO,FPS,RPG,RTS,ColorPresetsRGB,ColorPresetPanelNative,ColorPresetCinema,ColorPresetWarm,ColorPresetStandardNeutral,ColorPresetCool,ColorPresetGaming,ColorPresetVivid,ColorPresetCustomColor,ColorPresetHPEnhancePlus,ColorPresetLowBlueLight,Reading,TrNight,TrDicom,TrDicom0,TrDicom2,Text,Custom,MovieorCinema,LegacyPhoto,LegacyGaming,LegacyHPEnhancePlus,LegacyLowBlueLight,LegacyReading,LegacyNight,LegacyTechnicolor,LegacyMultimedia,LegacyWarm,LegacyNeutralorStandard,LegacyCool,Full,sRGB,Rec709BT709,AdobeRGB,Rec601,DCIP3,Custom1,Custom2,Custom3,Custom4,Custom5,Custom6,Custom7,DCIM,Custom1,Custom2,Custom3,Custom4,Custom5,Custom6,Custom7,DCIM
Specifies the contrast to set to the display. Valid values are between 0 and 100. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer.
Specifies the resolution to set to the display. Format of string 1234x1234 where two integers are separated by 'x' and the first integer is the width and the second integer is the height.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the device bridge file transfer function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the ethernet port menu. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Specifies the frequency in Hz to set the display. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer.

Specifies the language to set to the display. Valid values are: ChineseTraditional, English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, PortuguesePortugal, Russian, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish, ChineseSimplified, PortugueseBrazil, Arabic, Bulgarian, Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, Estonian, Finnish, Greek, Hebrew, Hindi, Hungarian, Latvian, Lituanian, Norwegian, Polish, Romanian, Serbian, Slovak, Slovenian, Thai, Ukrainian, Vietnamese
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the microphone function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
If set to false, speaker mute value will be set to 'UnMute'. If set to true, speaker mute value will be set to 'Mute'.
Specifies the power management value to set to the display. Valid values are: PowerSaverMode, PerformanceMode
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) presence detection menu. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Specifes the response time value to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, Level1, Level2, Level3, Level4, Level5, Level6
If set to false, the display will not restrict file transfer. If set to true, the display will restrict file transfer.
Specifies the serial number(s) of the display(s) to apply changes to. If not specified, all connected displays will be updated.
Allows the IT adminstrator to disable (lock) speaker function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.

Specifies the volume level to set to the display's speaker. Valid values are between 0 and 100. If a decimal value is specified, it will be rounded to the nearest integer. If connected via USBC, SpeakerVolume will follow SystemVolume despite value specified by SpeakerVolume parameter.
Specifies the volume level to set to the system. Display volume is synced with the host when connected via USB. Valid values are between 0 and 100.
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the USB Hub. Valid values are Disable and Enable.

Specifies the USB-C port to set to the display. Valid values are: USBCVideoUSB20, USBCVideoUSB30, USBCVideoUSB32, USB3840216060HzUSB20, USB3840216030HzUSB30, USB2560144060HzUSB20, USB2560144060HzUSB30, USB34401440100HzUSB20, USB3440144060HzUSB30, USB1920120060HzUSB20, USB1920120060HzUSB30, USB5120216060HzUSB20, USB5120216060HzUSB30, USB3840216060HzUSB30, USBCAUTO.

Specifies the split screen value to set to the display. Valid values are: SplitScreenOff, PbP, DeviceBridge, VirtualDualDisplay, PiP, PbP1_4, PbP3_4, VMD1, VDD1_4, VDD3_4, VMD2, ToggleSplitScreenOnorOff, ToggleBetweenWSplitScreenOptions, SwapLeftorRightSplitScreenInputs, ResetSplitScreenMenu, PbP1_3, PbP3_1
Allows the IT administrator to disable (lock) the webcam function. Valid values are Disable and Enable.
Specifies the switch USB value to set to the display. Valid values are: Default, Off, On
Specifies the value of the single power on message to set to the display. Valid values are: Off, On
Updates firmware on connected displays if available

This command updates firmware on connected displays if available. Please note that daisy chained devices are not currently supported. Prerequisites: * Close all running applications to avoid installation issues.
* Keep the monitor's power cable connected during the update process.
* Ensure the monitor is powered ON and displaying active video throughout the update.
* Avoid putting the computer system into sleep or shutdown mode during the update.
* Do not connect more than one computer system to the target monitor during the update.
* The monitor must be connected to the computer using a cable (USB-B to A cable, USB-C to C cable, USB-C to A cable, Thunderbolt cable) for the update to function properly.
Specifies the serial number(s) of the display(s) to update. If not specified, all connected displays will be updated.

Specifies whether update should run silently or interactively. If not specified, default is silent. If the display does not yet support silent install, an error will be thrown. Please note that this parameter cannot be used with the -Stage parameter.
Specifies the serial number(s) of the display(s) to update. If not specified, all connected displays will be updated.
Specifies the interval in minutes for checking the signal status. The default is 0 minutes.
If specified, the command will try and apply disconnected updates if supported and available. This parameter cannot be used with the -Experience parameter.
Specifies how long in minutes after signal loss to intiate the disconnected firmware update. The default is 10 minutes.
Specifies the serial number(s) of the display(s) to update. If not specified, all connected displays will be updated.

Specifies whether update should run silently or interactively. If not specified, default is silent. If the display does not yet support silent install, an error will be thrown. Please note that this parameter cannot be used with the -Stage parameter.
Specifies the interval in minutes for checking the signal status. The default is 0 minutes.
If specified, the command will try and apply disconnected updates if supported and available. This parameter cannot be used with the -Experience parameter.
Specifies how long in minutes after signal loss to intiate the disconnected firmware update. The default is 10 minutes.
